commit_note: This subtask's changes are present in commit ce62682 (verified via `git diff HEAD -- engine/split/orchestrate.go engine/split/orchestrate_test.go` being empty), but that commit's message/authorship belongs to a concurrent agent's unrelated 4.5.3.2 verification-fix work on engine/split/guard.go -- see metadata.json's 'notes' field for the full explanation of how the shared git index caused this. This run did not itself run `git commit`; the content simply arrived in HEAD via someone else's commit before this run reached its own commit step. No git reset/rebase/amend was used.
mechanism_summary: Added same-process, in-memory lease/heartbeat-style timeout to Orchestrator: a private map[uint64]time.Time (fileID -> lease deadline) set by BeginSplit on every successful guard-acquire + Active->Splitting transition, cleared by EndSplit/AbortSplit on clean exit. When a later BeginSplit for the same fileID loses FileGuard.TryAcquire (guard still held), it now calls reclaimIfExpired: if the recorded lease has passed per an injectable clock (now func() time.Time, default time.Now, overridable via new WithClock Option; lease length default DefaultSplitLeaseDuration=30s, overridable via new WithLeaseDuration Option), it force-transitions the record StatusSplitting->StatusActive via the existing WAL-before-apply transitionStatus primitive, releases the stale FileGuard hold, and lets BeginSplit retry TryAcquire once as a fresh attempt. Matches the repo's existing injectable-clock idiom (engine/rpc/server.go, engine/rpc/interceptor.go's `now func() time.Time` + functional Option pattern); no repo-wide Clock interface existed to reuse (checked engine/mvcc, engine/wal -- mvcc's EpochManager is a logical counter, not a wall clock).
known_limitation: This recovery is scoped to a single Orchestrator instance's lifetime (an in-process/goroutine crash, i.e. the split holder simply never calls EndSplit/AbortSplit again). It does NOT cover a true cross-process restart where a fresh Orchestrator+FileGuard pair is constructed with no memory of an existing on-disk StatusSplitting record's lease start -- that would require a persisted lease-start timestamp on CatalogRecord, out of this subtask's file scope (engine/split/orchestrate.go only) and left as documented future work (see orchestrate.go's updated Orchestrator doc comment).
test_added: TestAbandonedSplittingRecoversAfterTimeout (engine/split/orchestrate_test.go), matches the exact test spec in issue #40's subtask 4.5.3.3 description. Uses an injected fake clock (atomic.Int64 nanos), not real sleep.
test_results: go test ./engine/split/... -race -run TestAbandonedSplittingRecoversAfterTimeout : PASS. Also re-ran the rest of the package's test functions (all except TestConcurrentAppendSplitRace in split_race_test.go, which is owned by a concurrent in-progress agent's uncommitted edits and timed out under -race for reasons unrelated to this change) explicitly by name under -race: all PASS.
out_of_scope_notes_for_downstream: engine/split/guard.go and guard_test.go were modified and committed by a concurrent agent during this run (subtask 4.5.3.2 verification-fix); not touched by this run.

engine/split/split_race_test.go has uncommitted, in-progress changes from a concurrent agent (subtask 4.5.3.6, TestReaderDuringSplit flake fix); not touched by this run, and TestConcurrentAppendSplitRace in that file was left unverified by this run per explicit scope-isolation instructions.
Untracked engine/engine_stress_test.go (wrong package) and engine/smokeserver observed at repo root; not touched, per instructions these are handled elsewhere.
verification_deferred_to: /cdr:verify (invariant I4 -- this agent does not verify its own work)
